subject: Physics
title: Einstein's Miracle Year
description: A fast breakdown of Einstein's Miracle Year for builders.

hook.text: 1905 is called Einstein's miracle year. He published four papers, each worth a Nobel Prize.

buildup.text: Paper one explained the photoelectric effect—light comes in packets called photons.

discovery.text: Paper two proved atoms exist by explaining why pollen grains jitter in water.

twist.text: Paper three introduced special relativity. Paper four gave us E = mc². All in one year.

climax.text: No scientist before or since has matched that output. It was physics' greatest year.

punchline.text: Four papers. One year. The universe decoded.

quiz.question: How many groundbreaking papers did Einstein publish in 1905?
